title: Daily Promo Spot — Color-Graded Key Frames, Thumbnail Pack & Sizzle Reel
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the real brief: a daily promotional-video turnaround for a public-facing audience where the client supplies "fresh promotional footage" via shared drive, wants "tasteful color correction," "the occasional caption or lower-third," a polished 1080p+ deliverable, and same-day consistency with a "lively and accessible…nothing overly corporate" style. ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-connector benchmark: the brief's core ask (full Premiere timeline edit — trim/sequence/transitions/MP4 export + editable project) is NOT an Adobe-connector capability (video tools are async-widget [A]; video_resize is same-length-no-trim; quick_cut is AI-sizzle not timestamp trimming; no editable-project export). So I keep faithful to the brief's stated, connector-supported sub-tasks and reframe the DELIVERABLE as the same-day "promo package" a colorist/finisher actually hands back alongside the cut: a consistent color-grade applied to the client's exported key frames (matches category color_grade_lr and the brief's "tasteful color correction" + "lively, accessible, not corporate" look), a YouTube/social thumbnail derived from the hero frame (the brief's public-facing posting context), and a short engagement-cut sizzle + content summary for the post copy. The daily-folder return ("drop the finished files back in the folder") becomes the asset-folder packaging steps. No invented unrelated deliverable; every enrichment maps to a sentence in the brief.
one_line_ask: Take today's exported promo key frames, apply one consistent lively color grade across the frames, build a YouTube thumbnail from the hero shot, generate a sizzle cut and a content summary, and drop the finished, organized package back in the daily folder — same day.
full_brief: Fresh promotional footage lands in the shared drive each afternoon and the finished, post-ready package must go back in the same folder within 24 hours. For today's spot the client exported five representative key frames from the raw clips (hero close-up, two mid-shots, an environment/wide, and a product/detail shot), all shot in mixed afternoon lighting so they look uneven and slightly cool. Deliver a same-day "promo finishing package": (1) all five key frames carrying ONE consistent, lively-but-tasteful grade — auto-tone baseline, warmer temperature, lifted shadows, controlled highlights, a touch more vibrance, plus the same Lightroom look-preset on every frame so the set feels edited together and "accessible, not corporate"; (2) a 1920x1080 YouTube/social thumbnail built from the graded hero frame (subject popped from the busy background via a blurred-background treatment, then cropped to 16:9 with the subject weighted left so a title can sit right); (3) a 15-second vertical-friendly sizzle cut from the source clips for the social teaser, plus a short auto-generated content summary to seed the post caption/description. Package the graded frames, thumbnail and sizzle into a dated daily-delivery folder in CC and confirm done. Keep the turnaround fast and the look consistent across every frame.

inputs.name: keyframe_hero_closeup.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal still frame exported from a promotional video: candid close-up of a mid-30s small-business owner laughing mid-sentence in a sunlit artisan coffee roastery, shot on a cinema camera at 1920x1080, mixed afternoon window light that reads slightly cool and uneven, real skin pores and flyaway hairs, shallow depth of field with a busy blurred shelf-and-equipment background, documentary not glossy, natural grain.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like a frame-grab (slight motion-softness, 16:9 native, candid expression), not a posed studio portrait. Cool/uneven white balance is intentional so the grade has something to fix.

inputs.name: keyframe_mid_A.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal 1920x1080 video key frame: medium shot of the same coffee-roastery owner pouring beans from a scoop, mixed cool afternoon light, busy real-world background, candid documentary style, natural skin texture and grain, slightly underexposed shadows.
inputs.realism_notes: Same scene/subject and same cool, slightly-underexposed look as the hero so a single grade can unify all five frames.

inputs.name: keyframe_mid_B.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal 1920x1080 video key frame: medium shot of two staff members chatting over a roasting machine in the same coffee roastery, mixed cool window light, real textures, candid, natural grain, uneven exposure.
inputs.realism_notes: Different framing but identical lighting character; tests grade consistency across frames.

inputs.name: keyframe_wide_environment.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal 1920x1080 video key frame: wide environment shot of the artisan coffee roastery interior with customers, large windows blowing out slightly, cool afternoon cast, documentary, natural grain.
inputs.realism_notes: Brighter highlights here so the highlight-recovery step has work to do; same color cast for unity.

inputs.name: keyframe_product_detail.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal 1920x1080 video key frame: macro detail of a finished latte with rosetta art on a wooden counter in the same roastery, shallow depth of field, cool mixed light, real foam micro-texture, natural grain.
inputs.realism_notes: Detail/product shot completes the 5-frame set; same cool cast for consistent grading.

inputs.name: source_clips_for_sizzle.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: 1080p promotional b-roll montage of the same artisan coffee roastery: beans pouring, espresso pulling, latte art, owner laughing, customers chatting, warm natural light, handheld documentary feel, ~40 seconds of varied shots.
inputs.realism_notes: Longer multi-shot source so the AI sizzle cut has varied moments to pick from; 1080p native, not 720p/fast.

outputs.name: graded_keyframes/ (5 files)
outputs.spec: 5x 1920x1080 JPEG/PNG, each carrying the identical grade chain (auto-tone -> warmer temperature -> lifted shadows -> recovered highlights -> +vibrance -> same Lightroom look-preset), applied identically to all five frames (hero + 4). Visually consistent 'edited-together' lively look.

outputs.name: youtube_thumbnail_1920x1080.png
outputs.spec: 1920x1080 (16:9) PNG thumbnail from the graded hero frame: subject popped via the masked Adaptive 'Subject Pop / Blur Background' preset (subject stays sharp, busy background blurred), then cropped with subject weighted left, title-safe negative space on the right.

outputs.name: sizzle_15s_teaser.mp4
outputs.spec: ~15s AI engagement-cut sizzle reel assembled from the source clips for the social teaser (async-widget result in product).

outputs.name: content_summary.txt
outputs.spec: Short auto-generated scene summary of the source montage to seed the post caption + description.

outputs.name: daily_delivery_YYYY-MM-DD/
outputs.spec: Dated CC asset folder containing all of the above, organized for same-day handback.

connector_workflow.note: Ingest the client-supplied files dropped in the shared drive: the 5 key frames and the source montage MP4. Initializes the upload session to CC storage.
Finalize the upload so every input has a stable CC asset id / URI to feed the chain (5 frames + montage).
Start the grade spine on the hero frame: neutral auto-tone baseline to even out the uneven mixed-light exposure before the creative grade.
Push the cool afternoon cast warmer for the 'lively, accessible, not corporate' feel the brief asks for.
Lift the crushed shadows from the slightly-underexposed grab so detail returns without flattening contrast.
Pull back the blown window highlights so the brighter areas stay controlled — completes the tonal correction.
Add a touch of vibrance for the lively look while protecting skin tones.

Stamp the chosen Lightroom look-preset (the consistent 'house' look) on the hero. This same preset + the same temp/shadow/highlight/vibrance settings get repeated on the other four frames (steps 9-12) so the set reads as edited together. Produces graded frame 1 of 5.

Apply the exact same grade recipe established on the hero (auto-tone baseline + warmer temp + lifted shadows + recovered highlights + vibrance + the same Lightroom look-preset, batched into one adjustments call) to keyframe_mid_A so it matches. Graded frame 2 of 5.
Apply the identical grade recipe to keyframe_mid_B for a consistent 'edited-together' look. Graded frame 3 of 5.
Apply the identical grade recipe to keyframe_wide_environment (the blown-highlight frame benefits most from the shared highlight recovery). Graded frame 4 of 5.
Apply the identical grade recipe to keyframe_product_detail, completing the 5-frame graded set. Graded frame 5 of 5.
On the graded hero, select the subject (business owner) by prompt to build the mask that drives the subject-pop treatment.

Apply the masked Adaptive 'Subject Pop / Blur Background' preset: the preset uses the subject mask to keep the subject sharp while blurring the busy background, popping the subject for a clickable thumbnail. Consumes the graded hero + the subject mask (masked-preset path, not whole-image lens_blur).
Crop/resize the popped hero to exactly 1920x1080 (16:9) with the subject weighted left, leaving title-safe space on the right. Final thumbnail deliverable.
AI engagement-cut a ~15s social sizzle from the ingested source montage for the teaser. Async-widget; consumes the ingested montage asset.
Summarize the source montage's scenes to seed the post caption/description copy. Async-widget; consumes the ingested montage.
Create the dated daily-delivery folder in CC storage for the same-day handback.
Copy all finished deliverables (all 5 graded frames, thumbnail, sizzle, summary) into the dated folder — the 'drop finished files back in the folder' handback step.

chaining_note: Single output->input spine with one shared ingest root. Steps 1-2 ingest the client files -> ingested_asset_ids. The color-grade spine runs strictly in series on the hero frame: ingested_asset_ids -> auto_tone -> temperature -> dark_portions -> highlights -> vibrance -> apply_preset = hero_graded_final (steps 3-8), each consuming the immediately prior output. The exact same recipe established on the hero is then applied to the other four frames via one batched image_apply_adjustments call each (steps 9-12, each consuming its own ingested frame) -> midA/midB/wide/product_graded_final, so all five frames are actually costed and consistent. The thumbnail branch consumes hero_graded_final: select_by_prompt builds hero_subject_mask (step 13), then image_apply_preset applies the masked Adaptive 'Subject Pop / Blur Background' preset consuming BOTH hero_graded_final + hero_subject_mask -> hero_subject_popped (step 14; masked-preset path, NOT whole-image lens_blur) -> crop_and_resize -> youtube_thumbnail_1920x1080 (step 15). quick_cut (16) and summarize (17) each consume the shared ingested montage. Finally create_folders (18) -> copy_assets (19) consumes the folder plus every produced deliverable — all five graded frames, thumbnail, sizzle, summary — to assemble the dated handback. Every post-ingest step sets inputs_from to a prior step's named output.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon and wide-mix: 19 connector calls, all server=adobe, 15 distinct Adobe tools across three groups (image color/FX, asset/storage, video). It demands a real colorist decision chain (auto-tone baseline → temperature → shadow/highlight balance → vibrance → look-preset) held CONSISTENT across a 5-frame set, a masked subject-pop thumbnail (select_by_prompt → masked Subject-Pop preset → 16:9 crop), an AI sizzle cut, content summarization, and a packaged dated handback. It stays strictly inside connector limits: no Premiere timeline edit / project-file export, no trim-to-timestamp (video tools are tagged [A]; video_resize unused since it can't trim), image_fill_area avoided (solid-only), no generative fill / object removal / text-to-image / compositing — the only 'generative' touch (reframe-crop) is not used. Difficulty comes from sequencing a faithful, repeatable grade and respecting which sub-tasks are headless [C] vs async-widget [A].

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capability used. image_crop_and_resize/reframe-crop avoided; no upscale/super-resolution, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art/logo, no object-removal-with-reconstruction. image_fill_area not used. Canvas never grown: thumbnail is a 16:9 CROP of the graded hero via image_crop_and_resize (no invented pixels). The lens_blur+mask contradiction is removed: background blur uses the masked Adaptive 'Subject Pop / Blur Background' preset via image_apply_preset (whole-image lens_blur no longer given a mask). All 15 tools are real Adobe-connector tools.
reverify.notes: The remaining workflow is visual/video only. media_summarize is retained because it summarizes the video montage's scenes (not audio) to seed post copy, and content_summary is a text deliverable derived from the video. Steps renumbered 1-19; asset_copy_assets no longer references the deleted music/VO outputs. tool_call_count 22->19; distinct_adobe_tools 18->15 (dropped asset_search, asset_license_and_download_stock, media_enhance_speech); tools_used reconciled. Prior grade/thumbnail fixes preserved: the 4 non-hero graded frames are actually costed (4x image_apply_adjustments, steps 9-12), the masked-preset subject pop replaces the lens_blur+mask contradiction, and step 19 copies all five graded frames plus the thumbnail, sizzle and summary into the dated handback.
